channel.title: NPR Programs: Morning Edition
channel.description: Morning Edition takes listeners around the country and the world with multi-faceted stories and commentaries every weekday. Hosts Steve Inskeep, Leila Fadel, Michel Martin and A Martínez bring you the latest breaking news and features to prepare you for the day.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Former Maine Sen. Troy Jackson says it would be 'self-serving' if Graham Platner runs
item.description: Former Maine state Sen. Troy Jackson has filed to replace Graham Platner on the U.S. Senate ballot in Maine after Platner's former girlfriend accused him of sexual assault.
item.content:encoded:
Former Maine state Sen. Troy Jackson has filed to replace Graham Platner on the U.S. Senate ballot in Maine after Platner's former girlfriend accused him of sexual assault.

(Image credit: Graeme Sloan)
item.dc:creator: A Martínez

item.title: Trump meets with Zelenskyy, says the ceasefire with Iran is over
item.description: President Trump is now declaring the ceasefire between the US and Iran is over, and he's threatening to start striking Iran again tonight.
item.content:encoded: President Trump is now declaring the ceasefire between the US and Iran is over, and he's threatening to start striking Iran again tonight.
item.dc:creator: Deepa Shivaram

item.title: Calls for Graham Platner to drop out of Maine Senate race continue to grow
item.description: Graham Platner hasn't dropped out of Maine's U.S. Senate race, but the pressure continues to mount on him to do so, as Maine Democrats make a plan to choose a new candidate in the next two weeks.
item.content:encoded: Graham Platner hasn't dropped out of Maine's U.S. Senate race, but the pressure continues to mount on him to do so, as Maine Democrats make a plan to choose a new candidate in the next two weeks.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Miller

item.title: Democratic strategist Maria Cardona on calls for Graham Platner to drop out of race
item.description: NPR's A Martinez speaks with Democratic strategist Maria Cardona about what choices Democrats face amid allegations surrounding Senate candidate Graham Platner.
item.content:encoded: NPR's A Martinez speaks with Democratic strategist Maria Cardona about what choices Democrats face amid allegations surrounding Senate candidate Graham Platner.
item.dc:creator: A Martínez

item.title: Investigator details surveillance of Tyler Robinson on the day Charlie Kirk was shot
item.description: Prosecutors are making their case against Tyler Robinson, the suspected killer of Charlie Kirk, on charges that could lead to the death penalty. Robinson's defense team strongly objects.
item.content:encoded: Prosecutors are making their case against Tyler Robinson, the suspected killer of Charlie Kirk, on charges that could lead to the death penalty. Robinson's defense team strongly objects.
item.dc:creator: Sean Higgins

item.title: Morning news brief
item.description: Trump says ceasefire between the U.S. and Iran is over, pressure mounts for Graham Platner to drop out of Maine Senate race, IOC opens door for Russian athletes to compete in 2028 Games.
item.content:encoded: Trump says ceasefire between the U.S. and Iran is over, pressure mounts for Graham Platner to drop out of Maine Senate race, IOC opens door for Russian athletes to compete in 2028 Games.
item.dc:creator: Michel Martin

item.title: Former interior secretary Deb Haaland talks about her new memoir, 'A Voice Like Mine'
item.description: NPR's A Martínez speaks with former interior secretary and Democratic congresswoman from New Mexico, Deb Haaland, about her new memoir "A Voice Like Mine."
item.content:encoded: NPR's A Martínez speaks with former interior secretary and Democratic congresswoman from New Mexico, Deb Haaland, about her new memoir "A Voice Like Mine."
item.dc:creator: A Martínez

item.title: World Cup venues must hide logos and ads. But it's giving some brands more attention
item.description: World Cup venues must cover up all branding for companies that aren't FIFA sponsors, all the way down to the logos on the stadium seats. NPR's A Martinez speaks to marketing professor Andrew Rohm.
item.content:encoded: World Cup venues must cover up all branding for companies that aren't FIFA sponsors, all the way down to the logos on the stadium seats. NPR's A Martinez speaks to marketing professor Andrew Rohm.
item.dc:creator: A Martínez

item.title: Questions over AI campaign parodies rise as regulations struggle to control it
item.description: The regulation of AI use in Michigan and other states may not make a difference in what people see, raising questions over whether AI campaign parodies are political satire or something darker.
item.content:encoded: The regulation of AI use in Michigan and other states may not make a difference in what people see, raising questions over whether AI campaign parodies are political satire or something darker.
item.dc:creator: Colin Jackson

item.title: Ohio entrepreneur wants to bring 'farm-to-table' concept to clothing industry
item.description: The farm-to-table movement has been around for decades. An Ohio entrepreneur wants to replicate its success with clothing by building a regional textile economy.
item.content:encoded: The farm-to-table movement has been around for decades. An Ohio entrepreneur wants to replicate its success with clothing by building a regional textile economy.
item.dc:creator: Amanda Pirani
